Gauld Practice Financialsv3

Production · Collections · Insurance · Patient Reporting
442 claim-mo · 5,643 cpt-mo
across 2,691 unique patients
CLAIM · CPT · PAYMENT-TYPE merged
v3 changes (per client feedback): (1) OLD - DO NOT USE facilities now correctly mapped to Marietta location, recovering ~5 years of historical Marietta data. (2) New Production Reconciliation tab with monthly cascade explaining the gap between billed and collected. (3) New Payments by Insurance tab using the Payment Type Dashboard data.

Revenue Waterfall — Billed to Collected
Reading left to right: usual & customary charges are first reduced by contract-negotiated discounts (the largest single deduction), then by uncollectables and refunds. The remainder is collected — minus any amount still sitting in A/R for in-flight claims.

Production Reconciliation
The detail behind the production-vs-collections disparity. Insurance contract rates are typically 40–60% below usual & customary, which is the single largest reason billed and collected diverge.
Billed Charges
$45.56M
Contractual Adj
$24.37M
53.5% of billed
Other Adj + Refunds
$309.9K
writeoff + withheld + refunds
Net Production
$20.87M
billed − all adjustments
A/R Gap
$6.02M
still to collect
What the columns mean. Billed = usual & customary charges (production). Contractual Adj = insurance contract discount, the difference between U&C rate and the rate insurance actually pays under contract. Writeoff Adj = uncollectable balances written off. Payer Withheld = withholdings (e.g. quality-program holdbacks). Refunds = cash returned to patients/payers. Net Production = Billed − Adjustments − Refunds. Collected = actual cash received. A/R Gap = Net Production − Collected (services rendered but not yet paid).

Adjustment Composition

What's reducing billed to net production

Collection Rate Over Time

Every row balances: Billed − Contractual − Writeoff − Withheld − Refunds = Net Production. Net Production − Collected = A/R Gap.
